name: Somalia

visa_free.notes: Holders of ordinary passports are granted visa-free entry into Somalia. No max stay days confirmed on official source; some aggregators cite 30 days but this is unconfirmed from a live official page.
Holders of ordinary passports are granted visa-free entry into Somalia. No max stay days confirmed on official source; some aggregators cite 30 days but this is unconfirmed from a live official page.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Holders of ordinary passports are eligible for visa on arrival. No max stay days confirmed on official source; some aggregators cite 30 days but this is unconfirmed from a live official page.
Holders of ordinary passports are eligible for visa on arrival. No max stay days confirmed on official source; some aggregators cite 30 days but this is unconfirmed from a live official page.

Holders of ordinary passports are eligible for visa on arrival per etas.gov.so. A bilateral Immigration MoU was signed February 2026 (Somalia's Interior Minister and Tanzania's Home Affairs Minister) that reportedly provides 24-hour visa processing for ordinary passport holders — this does not change the existing VOA classification and is consistent with the pre-existing VOA arrangement. Diplomatic-holder visa-free access reported by non-official sources but unconfirmed from a live official page; tracked in gaps.

e_visa.notes: All visitors not listed under visa-free or visa-on-arrival categories must apply for eTAS (Electronic Travel Authorisation for Somalia) online before departure via etas.gov.so. The eTAS is a single-entry permit valid from the stated arrival date; entry must occur within 180 days of the arrival date. Effective September 1, 2025. NOTE: Taiwan (TWN) nationals are included in this eTAS category — a temporary entry ban on Taiwanese nationals was imposed in April 2025 and lifted in June 2025; they now require eTAS like all other non-exempt nationalities (confirmed via Wikipedia citing official Somalia immigration sources).

visa_policy.notes: Somalia's Immigration and Citizenship Agency (ICA) launched the mandatory eTAS (Electronic Travel Authorisation for Somalia) system on September 1, 2025, replacing the previous evisa.gov.so portal. All travelers must apply online at etas.gov.so before departure, except those holding nationality of Rwanda or Malaysia (visa-free), or 11 listed countries eligible for visa on arrival. Travelers holding multiple-entry visas, residence permits, or UN Laissez-Passer are also exempt from eTAS. The etas.gov.so data is as of June 2026 per official site. NOTE: Prior record cited passport_types as diplomatic/service/ordinary for visa-free and visa-on-arrival; corrected to ordinary only - the notes regarding diplomatic passports for those nationality entries were inaccurate given that diplomatic-only waivers are tracked separately under conditional_access.

conditional_access.conditions: Traveler must hold a valid multiple-entry visa issued by any country. Exempt from eTAS (Electronic Travel Authorisation for Somalia) requirement per ICA/SCAA AIC A 10/2025, effective 3 September 2025. Issuer country and visa class not restricted by official text.

Traveler must hold a valid residence permit issued by any country. Exempt from eTAS (Electronic Travel Authorisation for Somalia) requirement per ICA/SCAA AIC A 10/2025, effective 3 September 2025. Issuer country not restricted by official text.
Traveler must hold a valid United Nations Laissez-Passer (UNLP). Exempt from eTAS (Electronic Travel Authorisation for Somalia) requirement per ICA/SCAA AIC A 10/2025, effective 3 September 2025.

Mutual visa exemption for diplomatic passport holders under Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) signed at the sidelines of the 19th Intersessional Meeting of the Non-Aligned Movement (NAM) Foreign Ministers in Kampala. Date of signing: October 2025 (described as 'Wednesday' in official text; exact date not stated). Ordinary passport holders are NOT covered.

Visa waiver agreement abolishing visa requirements for holders of diplomatic passports, signed in Islamabad, February 2026 (Cabinet approved March 5, 2026). Witnessed by Pakistani President H.E. Asif Ali Zardari. Signed by Pakistan's Special Secretary (Ministry of Interior and Narcotics Control) and Somalia's Permanent Secretary (Ministry of Foreign Affairs). Ordinary passport holders are NOT covered.

Mutual visa waiver agreement signed 23 January 2025 in Cairo by Somalia's Minister of Foreign Affairs H.E. Ahmed Moallim Fiqi Ahmed and Egypt's Minister of Foreign Affairs H.E. Dr. Badr Abdelatty. Allows diplomatic passport holders to enter, exit, transit, and reside without restrictions for up to 90 days. Approved by Somali Cabinet (chaired by PM Hamza Abdi Barre). Note: Egyptian ordinary passport holders are already eligible for visa on arrival under the separate eTAS framework.

Mutual visa-waiver agreement for holders of diplomatic passports signed 28 November 2025 in Rabat by Somalia's Minister of Foreign Affairs H.E. Abdisalam Abdi Ali and Morocco's Minister of Foreign Affairs H.E. Nasser Bourita, alongside a broader 2026-2028 cooperation roadmap. No max stay days stated in official text. Ordinary passport holders are NOT covered.

Mutual visa exemption agreement for holders of diplomatic passports, concluded 2 April 2026 in Mogadishu during the second session of the Somalia-Sudan Political Consultation Committee. Co-chaired by Somalia's Permanent Secretary H.E. Hamza Adan Haadow and Sudan's Undersecretary H.E. Ambassador Muawiya Osman Khalid. No max stay days stated in official text. Ordinary passport holders are NOT covered.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to pass through Somalia en route to another destination.
visa_types.notes: One of seven visa categories issued by Somalia's Immigration Directorate. Visa on arrival is available to citizens of all countries; pre-approved visa letter can be processed within 48 hours and is valid 14 days from issuance. Most travelers now apply via eTAS at etas.gov.so.

visa_types.name: Business Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals travelling to Somalia for business activities, meetings, or commercial purposes.
visa_types.notes: One of seven visa categories issued by Somalia's Immigration Directorate. Pre-approval letter valid 14 days from issuance; applicants must provide passport, return ticket, two photos, and payment receipt at port of entry. Apply via eTAS at etas.gov.so or through a local representative.

visa_types.name: Tourism Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals visiting Somalia for tourism and leisure purposes.
visa_types.notes: One of seven visa categories issued by Somalia's Immigration Directorate. Pre-approval letter valid 14 days from issuance. For most nationalities, apply via eTAS (etas.gov.so). Visa on arrival available to citizens of listed African/Arab nations. Rwanda and Malaysia nationals are visa-free.

visa_types.name: Work Permit
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals who intend to take up employment or provide professional services in Somalia.
visa_types.notes: One of seven visa categories issued by Somalia's Immigration Directorate. Specific conditions, fees, and processing times not published on official sources as of research date. Applied through the Immigration Directorate.

visa_types.name: Education and Research Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals travelling to Somalia for educational study or academic research purposes.
visa_types.notes: One of seven visa categories issued by Somalia's Immigration Directorate. Specific conditions, fees, and processing times not published on official sources as of research date.

visa_types.name: Temporary Residence
visa_types.purpose: Grants foreign nationals the right to reside in Somalia on a temporary basis for an extended period.
visa_types.notes: One of seven visa categories issued by Somalia's Immigration Directorate. Holders of valid temporary residence permits are exempt from the eTAS (Electronic Travel Authorisation) requirement per SCAA AIC A 10/2025. Specific conditions and fees not published on official sources as of research date.

visa_types.name: Permanent Residence
visa_types.purpose: Grants foreign nationals the right to reside in Somalia indefinitely on a permanent basis.
visa_types.notes: One of seven visa categories issued by Somalia's Immigration Directorate. Holders of valid permanent residence permits are exempt from the eTAS (Electronic Travel Authorisation) requirement per SCAA AIC A 10/2025. Specific conditions and fees not published on official sources as of research date.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on any official Somalia government source. No program exists as of the research date.

agreements: IGAD Protocol on Free Movement of Persons (signed by Somalia; not yet in operative force as a free movement area as of June 2026)

validation_notes: VERIFIED (2026-06-14): India (IND) ordinary passport holders require eTAS (e_visa) to enter Somalia - NOT visa-free or visa-on-arrival. Only Rwanda and Malaysia are visa-free; only 11 listed African/Arab nations get visa-on-arrival. India falls under 'all other nationalities' eTAS category per etas.gov.so (official ICA portal) and usa.mfa.gov.so/how-to-apply-somali-evisa-online/. Aggregator claim of visa-free/visa-on-arrival for India is incorrect. No separate India entry added; existing e_visa catch-all entry covers India.
ADDED: Morocco diplomatic passport visa-waiver agreement (signed 28 Nov 2025 in Rabat, confirmed on web.mfa.gov.so). Was missing from prior record.

ADDED: Sudan diplomatic passport visa-waiver agreement (signed 2 April 2026 in Mogadishu, confirmed on web.mfa.gov.so). Previously listed only in gaps as unconfirmed non-official reports; now confirmed on official MFA source.

VERIFIED: All existing visa_policy entries (Rwanda, Malaysia visa-free; 11 visa-on-arrival nationalities; eTAS for all others) unchanged - confirmed via Wikipedia/Timatic cross-reference against etas.gov.so data (etas.gov.so itself returned HTTP 403 on direct fetch, but Wikipedia cites it as source for the same 11 + 2 list).
VERIFIED: Azerbaijan, Pakistan, Egypt, Morocco, Sudan diplomatic passport conditional_access entries confirmed against official MFA pages - no factual corrections needed.
VERIFIED: Qatar agreements (Dec 2025) cover customs, youth, agriculture, culture - no visa waiver component.
VERIFIED: Spain MoU (March 2026) is political consultations only - no visa waiver component.

VERIFIED: sominvest.gov.so visa-regime page (last modified 2022) pre-dates the eTAS system and says 'Visa on arrival is available to citizens of all countries with no exception' - this is superseded by the eTAS system and is correctly not used as the authoritative source for current policy.
UPDATED: Egypt conditional_access notes expanded to match primary signing-event MFA article language (enter, exit, transit, and reside).

CORRECTED (2026-06-27): Removed inaccurate language from visa_free and visa_on_arrival notes that stated 'diplomatic, service, or ordinary passports' - visa-free and visa-on-arrival access on etas.gov.so is by ordinary-passport nationality. Diplomatic-passport specific waivers are correctly tracked under conditional_access only.

VERIFIED (2026-06-27): No new ordinary-passport visa-free agreements found. Search for MFA news through June 2026 confirms all new agreements are diplomatic-passport-only. Pakistan Cabinet approval date clarified as March 5, 2026.

NOTE (2026-06-27): Some aggregators (visafreenomads.com, passportindex.org) cite 30-day max stay for visa-free and visa-on-arrival. etas.gov.so returned HTTP 403 on direct fetch so this could not be confirmed from a live official page. max_stay_days kept as null per safety rule; added to gaps.

NOTED (2026-06-29): Taiwan (TWN) nationals had a temporary entry ban imposed in April 2025, which was lifted in June 2025 (confirmed via Wikipedia citing official Somalia immigration sources). Taiwan nationals now require eTAS like all other non-exempt nationalities. No change to classified lists needed — Taiwan was never in visa-free or VOA lists; covered by the e_visa catch-all entry. Taiwan note added to e_visa entry.

NOTED (2026-06-29): Somalia-Tanzania Immigration MoU signed February 2026 (Somalia's Interior Minister Abdullahi Sheikh Ismail Fartaag and Tanzania's Home Affairs Minister Patrobas Paschal Katambi). Non-official sources report 24-hour visa processing for ordinary Tanzanian passport holders (consistent with pre-existing VOA) and visa-free for diplomatic holders. Tanzania already classified as VOA on etas.gov.so — no upgrade to visa-free for ordinary holders confirmed from official source. VOA classification preserved. Tanzania diplomatic-holder exemption NOT added to conditional_access (no official source confirmation). Added to gaps.

VERIFIED (2026-06-29): MFA news feed reviewed through June 2026 (web.mfa.gov.so/category/mfa-news/). No new ordinary-passport visa-free or VOA agreements found beyond existing record. All new 2026 bilateral agreements confirmed to be diplomatic-passport-only. Morocco and Sudan diplomatic agreements already in record. No new entries needed.
gaps: Max stay days for visa-free (Rwanda, Malaysia) and visa-on-arrival countries not confirmed from a live official page - etas.gov.so returns HTTP 403. Some aggregators cite 30 days but this is unconfirmed.

The eTAS exemption for 'multiple-entry visas' does not specify which issuing countries are accepted or whether the visa must be used/unused. Exact scope (e.g., does a Schengen C visa qualify) is not clarified on official source.
Azerbaijan MoU: exact date of signing in Kampala not stated in MFA official text; only described as 'Wednesday' at the NAM Intersessional in October 2025.
Pakistan agreement: exact date in February 2026 not stated in MFA official text; Cabinet approval was March 5, 2026.
Morocco agreement: max stay days not stated in official MFA text.
Sudan agreement: max stay days not stated in official MFA text.
No OCI or similar special diaspora/heritage status rules found on any official Somalia immigration source.
No RBI, fast-track, or talent visa programs found on official sources - absence confirmed but not explicitly stated by government.
IGAD Protocol on Free Movement of Persons signed by Somalia (confirmed igad.int) but not yet ratified into force; operational free movement not confirmed via official Somali government source.
Kuwait MoUs (August 2025) confirmed on MFA site but cover only joint committee and diplomatic institute cooperation - no visa waiver provision found in official text.

aip.scaa.gov.so/eAIC/HC-eAIC-2025-10-A-en-GB.html returned HTTP 403 on 2026-06-27 and 2026-06-29 re-fetches — conditional_access eTAS exemption entries (multiple-entry visa, residence permit, UNLP) preserved from prior confirmed fetch. No changes made per safety rule.

Somalia-Tanzania Immigration MoU (February 2026): non-official sources report ordinary Tanzanian holders get 24-hour visa processing (consistent with VOA, no upgrade) and diplomatic holders get visa-free. Cannot confirm diplomatic exemption from a live official page — MFA pages for Tanzania are pre-MoU. Tanzania diplomatic conditional_access entry NOT added. Pending official confirmation.

Taiwan (TWN): ban imposed April 2025 / lifted June 2025 confirmed via Wikipedia. eTAS portal (etas.gov.so) could not be fetched directly (HTTP 403); Taiwan's current eTAS classification unconfirmed from live official page but consistent with all available secondary sources.

outbound_official.note: no official Somalia government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. Checked: web.mfa.gov.so (MFA - no outbound list; only diplomatic news and bilateral MoUs), immigration.gov.so/en/visa/ (ICA - covers only inbound visas to Somalia, not outbound travel for Somali citizens), somalia.gov.so (main government portal - no travel/visa information for Somali nationals), ankara.mfa.gov.so and usa.mfa.gov.so embassy sites (consular services cover passport issuance and authentication, not outbound visa-free lists). Somalia MFA's 'Services for Citizens Abroad' section provides emergency travel documents and passport services only.
